compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (0 blocking, 4 advisory). Remediation: (1 critical) cross-phase data-flow entanglement — reconcile_once threads many locals (snapshot, binding_store, client, pass_id, mutation lists, manifest, alert state) across load->diff->apply->persist. Design: extract phase helpers that take/return EXPLICIT params (or a small in-file context) rather than relying on closure state, so each phase is independently testable; verify byte-for-byte behavior via the existing idempotency + corrupt-snapshot tests. (minor x3) ACs 'materially shorter'/'max nesting drops' lack thresholds — will report concrete before/after LOC + nesting; extracting 5 helpers at once is risky — will extract incrementally and run the reconcile suite after each. Branching off the unfed-liner-arson branch (both touch reconcile.py) to avoid a merge conflict.

compiled_state.description:
RISK 3 (worst single unit). `reconcile_once()` in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py:556-1315` is a ~760-LOC monolith coupling snapshot-load -> diff -> apply -> persist -> log plus corrupt-snapshot abort + OM->Mutation conversion. It is stable (not actively breaking) but untestable per-phase and the highest-touch reconciler file. Decompose IN-PLACE (the natural sub-blocks are below the 100-LOC file floor, so the module-size policy prescribes in-file helpers, NOT a new module).

## Why
No sub-phase can be tested or reused without invoking the whole orchestrator; 5-deep nested error handling lives in the spine. `_apply_batch` in applier.py (handled in the sibling Risk-2b ticket) is the related deep function.

## What — in-place helper extraction along existing seams
- Extract `_load_snapshots()`, `_run_differs()`, `_apply_mutations()`, `_persist_and_log()` as module-level (in-file) helpers; keep `reconcile_once()` as a thin sequencer that calls them in order.
- Lift `_handle_corrupt_snapshot()` out of the spine into its own helper.
- Preserve the single-pass IDEMPOTENT contract and the corrupt-snapshot abort semantics exactly.

## Scope
In: behavior-preserving in-place decomposition + per-phase unit tests now made possible. Out: creating a new file (sub-blocks are <100 LOC), changing the reconcile contract, touching the acli subprocess boundary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `reconcile_once()` body is a thin sequencer; the four phases + corrupt-snapshot handler are separate in-file helpers.
- [ ] At least the load/persist phases gain direct unit tests (previously impossible).
- [ ] reconcile.py stays in the allowlist if still >800, but `reconcile_once` itself is materially shorter and max nesting drops.
- [ ] Full reconcile idempotency + corrupt-snapshot-abort tests pass byte-for-byte.
compiled_state.title: Risk 3: decompose reconcile_once() into in-place phase helpers
